title: Helictotrichon leve
intro: Helictotrichon leve, also known as Blue Oat Grass, is a perennial grass species native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its distinctive blue-green foliage and tall, slender stems. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Helictotrichon leve include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Helictotrichon leve is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, colds, and respiratory infections.
